events.title: Israel-Lebanon Talks Held in Washington
events.desc: The United States is facilitating direct talks between Israel and Lebanon concerning border issues, the disarmament of Hezbollah, and ongoing Israeli military actions. This marks the third round of talks, with the US aiming for a comprehensive peace and security agreement contingent on Lebanese action against Hezbollah. However, the Lebanese government faces domestic challenges in agreeing to terms that could isolate it.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill 22 in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: A series of Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon, including Beirut, have resulted in the deaths of 22 people, among them eight children. Lebanon's health ministry confirmed that paramedics were also killed while on a rescue mission in Nabatieh following an earlier attack.

events.title: UN Aid Convoy Hit by Drones in Ukraine's Kherson
events.desc: A United Nations humanitarian convoy delivering aid to the city of Kherson in Ukraine was struck twice by drones. The incident highlights the ongoing risks to humanitarian operations in conflict zones.

events.title: US Admiral Claims Iran's Ability to Threaten Neighbors Dramatically Degraded
events.desc: Admiral Brad Cooper, head of U.S. Central Command, stated that U.S. bombings have significantly reduced Iran's capacity to threaten its neighbors and U.S. interests, setting back Iran's defense industry by 90%. He emphasized the tactical successes of the military campaign against Iran.

events.title: Iran's FM Urges BRICS to Condemn US-Israeli Aggression
events.desc: Iran's Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi, speaking at a gathering of foreign ministers in New Delhi, called upon BRICS states to condemn what he termed 'US-Israeli aggression'.

events.title: Hezbollah Support Endures in South Lebanon Amid Ceasefire Failures
events.desc: Despite ongoing Israeli attacks and occupation, many residents in southern Lebanon continue to support Hezbollah, viewing it as the primary force capable of defending them. This enduring support persists even as the ceasefire announced last month fails to halt hostilities with Israel.

events.title: 'Floating Armoury' Ship Reportedly Seized by Iran
events.desc: Reports indicate that a vessel described as a 'floating armoury' located in the Gulf of Oman has been seized by Iranian military personnel. The exact nature of the vessel and the circumstances of its seizure are not fully detailed.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 13 Killed in Israeli Strikes, Including Paramedics
events.desc: Lebanon's health ministry reported that 13 people were killed in Israeli strikes, including two paramedics who were on a rescue mission in Nabatieh. The incident occurred in response to an earlier attack that had claimed one life.

events.title: US House Narrowly Rejects Bid to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
events.desc: The U.S. House of Representatives narrowly defeated a Democratic-led resolution that sought to stop the Iran war unless authorized by Congress. The vote was 212-212, failing to secure the simple majority needed for passage, thus leaving President Trump's military campaign powers intact.

events.title: EU Agrees Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Over West Bank Violence
events.desc: The European Union has agreed to impose sanctions on Israeli settlers responsible for violence in the West Bank. The EU's foreign policy chief stated that 'extremism and violence carry consequences,' a move that Israel has branded as 'arbitrary.'

events.title: Israeli Nationalists Hold Parade in Jerusalem's Old City
events.desc: Thousands of Israeli nationalists marched through the Muslim quarter of Jerusalem's Old City under heavy security during the annual Jerusalem Day parade. The event marks Israel's capture of East Jerusalem and is seen by Palestinians as a provocation.

events.title: IMF Sees Risks to Global Growth Forecast Over Sustained Iran War
events.desc: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned that ongoing disruptions from the Iran war are pushing the global economic outlook towards an 'adverse' scenario, with reduced growth and increased inflation risks. The IMF had previously predicted global growth to drop to 3.1 percent in 2026.

events.title: Gaza Aid Flotilla Sets Sail Again After Israeli Interceptions
events.desc: Ships from the Global Sumud Flotilla have set sail for a third time from southern Turkey, aiming to deliver aid to Gaza. Previous attempts were intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters, leading to the detention of activists.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery indicates that at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within Israel's 'Yellow Line' in southern Lebanon have sustained heavy damage or complete destruction. Much of this demolition has occurred recently, despite a fragile ceasefire.

events.title: Egypt Farmers Hit by Iran War Price Surge
events.desc: Egyptian smallholder farmers are facing severe economic hardship due to soaring fertilizer and energy prices, exacerbated by the ongoing Iran war. Many are forced to lay off workers and reduce farming land, with some abandoning fertilizer-intensive crops like wheat.
